subject: GRB 240619B: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 11:10:47 UT on 19 Jun 2024, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 240619B (trigger 740488252.364386 / 240619466).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 116.0, Dec = 18.4 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 07h 44m, 18d 23'), with a statistical uncertainty of 20.9 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 68.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240619466/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn240619466.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240619466/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn240619466.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn240619466/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn240619466.gif
